rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type or implementation scope as required by the contract semantics
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks, not modifying component types or implementations
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when they relate to component type features
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes when they relate to subcomponent features
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the current visible identifiers list when available
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when they relate to component type features
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes when they relate to subcomponent features
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume` for input constraints and `guarantee` for output guarantees
Use `eq` for helper declarations and `const` for named constants
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced features are not visible
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
